♦
AI Security
Tue, 03 Mar 2026 11:09:30 GMT  •  Unit 42
Fooling AI Agents: Web-Based Indirect Prompt Injection Observed in the Wild
🚨 Indirect prompt injection (IDPI) is actively being weaponized, moving beyond theoretical risks.
💥 In-the-wild IDPI attacks include AI-based ad review evasion, SEO manipulation, data destruction, and unauthorized transactions.
👾 Researchers identified 22 distinct techniques used by attackers for web-based IDPI payloads in observed incidents.
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 13:37:58 GMT  •  OpenTools
Critical Vulnerability Exposes Perplexity's Comet Browser to Prompt Injection Attacks
🚨 Perplexity's Comet AI browser had a critical vulnerability, dubbed 'PleaseFix,' allowing indirect prompt injection attacks.
🔓 The 'PleaseFix' flaw permitted zero-click exploits, enabling the leakage of user files and sensitive data.
🔬 Researchers from Zenity Labs identified the 'PleaseFix' vulnerability in Perplexity's Comet AI browser.
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 05:00:00 GMT  •  Help Net Security
Engineering trust: A security blueprint for autonomous AI agents
🏥 AI agents accessing sensitive data, like health records, are vulnerable to malicious instructions hidden in webpages they process.
🛡️ The most significant security risk for autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, lies primarily at the application layer.
🔒 A secure-by-design blueprint for AI agents recommends an adversarial threat model and isolated sandboxed environments for code execution.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 14:30:25 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
What is AI Security? Top Security Risks in LLM Applications
📈 55% of organizations reported using AI in at least one business function in McKinsey’s 2023 Global AI Survey.
📉 Research shows 45% of AI-generated code contains security vulnerabilities.
⚠️ Prompt injection attacks effectively bypass safeguards in many LLM applications, leading to reported data leakage from generative AI tools.
2026-03-05
Benjamin Feuer, Lucas Rosenblatt, Oussama Elachqar
Towards Provably Unbiased LLM Judges via Bias-Bounded Evaluation
LLM-as-judge adversarial NIST taxonomy
As AI models progress beyond simple chatbots into more complex workflows, we draw ever closer to the event horizon beyond which AI systems will be utilized in autonomous, self-maintaining feedback loops. Any autonomous AI system will depend on automated, verifiable rewards and feedback; in settings where ground truth is sparse or non-deterministic, one practical source of such rewards is an LLM-as-a-Judge. Although LLM judges continue to improve, the literature has yet to introduce systems capable of enforcing standards with strong guarantees, particularly when bias vectors are unknown or adversarially discovered. To remedy this issue, we propose average bias-boundedness (A-BB), an algorithmic framework which formally guarantees reductions of harm/impact as a result of any measurable bias in an LLM judge. Evaluating on Arena-Hard-Auto with four LLM judges, we achieve (tau=0.5, delta=0.01) bias-bounded guarantees while retaining 61-99% correlation with original rankings across formatting and schematic bias settings, with most judge-bias combinations exceeding 80%. The code to reproduce our findings is available at https://github.com/penfever/bias-bounded-evaluation.
2026-03-05
Guo Chen, Lidong Lu, Yicheng Liu et al.
Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline
agentic AI
While datasets for video understanding have scaled to hour-long durations, they typically consist of densely concatenated clips that differ from natural, unscripted daily life. To bridge this gap, we introduce MM-Lifelong, a dataset designed for Multimodal Lifelong Understanding. Comprising 181.1 hours of footage, it is structured across Day, Week, and Month scales to capture varying temporal densities. Extensive evaluations reveal two critical failure modes in current paradigms: end-to-end MLLMs suffer from a Working Memory Bottleneck due to context saturation, while representative agentic baselines experience Global Localization Collapse when navigating sparse, month-long timelines. To address this, we propose the Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), which employs dynamic memory management to iteratively update a recursive belief state, significantly outperforming existing methods. Finally, we establish dataset splits designed to isolate temporal and domain biases, providing a rigorous foundation for future research in supervised learning and out-of-distribution generalization.
2026-03-05
Artem Vazhentsev (Independent Researcher), Maria Marina, Daniil Moskovskiy et al.
Leveraging LLM Parametric Knowledge for Fact Checking without Retrieval
agentic AI
Trustworthiness is a core research challenge for agentic AI systems built on Large Language Models (LLMs). To enhance trust, natural language claims from diverse sources, including human-written text, web content, and model outputs, are commonly checked for factuality by retrieving external knowledge and using an LLM to verify the faithfulness of claims to the retrieved evidence. As a result, such methods are constrained by retrieval errors and external data availability, while leaving the models intrinsic fact-verification capabilities largely unused. We propose the task of fact-checking without retrieval, focusing on the verification of arbitrary natural language claims, independent of their source. To study this setting, we introduce a comprehensive evaluation framework focused on generalization, testing robustness to (i) long-tail knowledge, (ii) variation in claim sources, (iii) multilinguality, and (iv) long-form generation. Across 9 datasets, 18 methods and 3 models, our experiments indicate that logit-based approaches often underperform compared to those that leverage internal model representations. Building on this finding, we introduce INTRA, a method that exploits interactions between internal representations and achieves state-of-the-art performance with strong generalization. More broadly, our work establishes fact-checking without retrieval as a promising research direction that can complement retrieval-based frameworks, improve scalability, and enable the use of such systems as reward signals during training or as components integrated into the generation process.
2026-03-05
Hugo Buurmeijer, Carmen Amo Alonso, Aiden Swann et al.
Observing and Controlling Features in Vision-Language-Action Models
safety NIST taxonomy
Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) have shown remarkable progress towards embodied intelligence. While their architecture partially resembles that of Large Language Models (LLMs), VLAs exhibit higher complexity due to their multi-modal inputs/outputs and often hybrid nature of transformer and diffusion heads. This is part of the reason why insights from mechanistic interpretability in LLMs, which explain how the internal model representations relate to their output behavior, do not trivially transfer to VLA counterparts. In this work, we propose to close this gap by introducing and analyzing two main concepts: feature-observability and feature-controllability. In particular, we first study features that are linearly encoded in representation space, and show how they can be observed by means of a linear classifier. Then, we use a minimal linear intervention grounded in optimal control to accurately place internal representations and steer the VLA's output towards a desired region. Our results show that targeted, lightweight interventions can reliably steer a robot's behavior while preserving closed-loop capabilities. We demonstrate on different VLA architectures ($π_{0.5}$ and OpenVLA) through simulation experiments that VLAs possess interpretable internal structure amenable to online adaptation without fine-tuning, enabling real-time alignment with user preferences and task requirements.
2026-03-05
Weijie Lyu, Ming-Hsuan Yang, Zhixin Shu
FaceCam: Portrait Video Camera Control via Scale-Aware Conditioning
NIST taxonomy
We introduce FaceCam, a system that generates video under customizable camera trajectories for monocular human portrait video input. Recent camera control approaches based on large video-generation models have shown promising progress but often exhibit geometric distortions and visual artifacts on portrait videos due to scale-ambiguous camera representations or 3D reconstruction errors. To overcome these limitations, we propose a face-tailored scale-aware representation for camera transformations that provides deterministic conditioning without relying on 3D priors. We train a video generation model on both multi-view studio captures and in-the-wild monocular videos, and introduce two camera-control data generation strategies: synthetic camera motion and multi-shot stitching, to exploit stationary training cameras while generalizing to dynamic, continuous camera trajectories at inference time. Experiments on Ava-256 dataset and diverse in-the-wild videos demonstrate that FaceCam achieves superior performance in camera controllability, visual quality, identity and motion preservation.

Source: NYC Open Data — Permitted Events
♦
Top News
Sat, 07 Mar 2026 11:30:06 +0000  •  Ars Technica
From Iran to Ukraine, everyone's trying to hack security cameras
📰 t targeted consumer-grade security cameras around the Middle East—with many apparently timed to Iran’s recent missile and drone strikes on targets that included Israel, Qatar, and Cyprus.
📢 Those camera-hijacking efforts, some of which Check Point has attributed to a hacker group that’s been previously linked to Iranian intelligence, suggest that Iran’s military has tried to use civilian surveillance cameras as a means to spot targets, plan strikes, or assess damage from its attacks as it retaliates for the US and Israeli bombings that have sparked a widening war in the region.
🔍 Iran wouldn’t be the first to adopt that camera-hacking surveillance tactic.
Sat, 07 Mar 2026 17:28:41 +0000  •  TechCrunch
OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
📰 OpenAI has delayed the launch of “adult mode,” a ChatGPT feature that will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content.
📢 OpenAI CEO Sam Altman first announced the feature in October, writing, “In December, as we roll out age-gating more fully and as part of our ‘treat adult users like adults’ principle, we will allow even more, like erotica for verified adults.
🔍 ” The launch had already been delayed once, from December — when Altman reportedly sent an internal memo declaring a “code red” and calling for teams to focus on the core ChatGPT experience — until the first quarter of this year.
Sat, 07 Mar 2026 10:45:00 GMT  •  Slate
The Senator Who Cost Kristi Noem Her Job
📰 e Iran war was (and is) the biggest of all of these, but we’re not leading the newsletter with it.
📢 Needs a little seasoning.
🔍 OK, one other thing happened in politics this week—the Montanans all went nuts—but that’s it.
Sat, 07 Mar 2026 14:02:37 -0500  •  NPR
Authorities searching debris after suspected tornadoes kill 6 in Michigan, Oklahoma
📰 Photos and videos posted on social media showed flattened homes and knocked down trees in a lakeside neighborhood.
📢 Severe thunderstorms that began in northern Indiana appeared to spawn multiple tornadoes in southern Michigan on Friday, said Lonnie Fisher, a meteorologist for the National Weather Service, which sent teams to the region Saturday to evaluate the damage and confirm tornadoes.
🔍 "Mostly likely there were three distinct tornadoes, but we won't know 100 percent for sure until they finish the survey," Fisher said Saturday, adding that the storms rapidly intensified in southern Michigan after hitting northern Indiana.
Sat, 07 Mar 2026 14:34:51 GMT  •  CNN
Dozens killed, authorities say, as Israeli commandos raid Lebanon in hunt for missing airman
📰 d the village of Nabi Chit, the Lebanese military said the commandos engaged in a firefight with Hezbollah militants and local residents.
📢 Hezbollah said in a statement that its fighters had fired on the “enemy infantry force” with “light and medium weapons” as the commandos neared a cemetery in the village’s eastern neighborhood.
🔍 The Iran-backed militant group said it carried out “concentrated fire” on the Israeli forces as they withdrew from the area.
♦
YouTube
2026-03-07  •  AI Daily Brief
Jensen Huang Calls OpenClaw "Most Important Software Release Ever"
🤖 Jensen Huang lauded OpenClaw as the "most important software release ever," signaling a shift towards new agent-era primitives such as persistent work, multimodal orchestration, and AI that follows users across devices.
⚔️ Anthropic's ongoing standoff with the Pentagon has reportedly escalated.
💨 OpenAI's ambitious Stargate project is currently experiencing turbulence.
2026-03-07  •  Dr. Izzy Sealey
Be Better than AI: 8 Highest Value Skills to Learn Now
⏳ Acknowledge the rapid decay of technical skills, with the half-life of relevance now significantly shorter than the previous 15 years, and prioritize developing irreplaceable human skills.
🗣️ Apply the four-step Nonviolent Communication (NVC) framework by observing without judgment, naming feelings, identifying unmet needs, and making clear, actionable requests for improved communication.
🤝 Build a personal brand by consistently creating "founder content," which generates 46% more engagement than corporate material and can attract opportunities, as demonstrated by Alex and Leila Hormozi's success with acquisition.com.
2026-03-07  •  Theo - t3.gg
It's finally here.
💻 Consider using the new, free, and open-source T3 Code desktop and web application to build software with AI agents, leveraging its support for various models and concurrent workflows via isolated work trees.
🚀 Streamline your development workflow by utilizing T3 Code's one-click GitHub integration, which can generate a commit message, commit changes, push to a branch, and create a pull request automatically.
💡 Learn from Julius's agentic engineering approach within T3 Code, which enabled him to log over 1,200 hours of coding in a single week according to Macroscope's dashboard, demonstrating extreme throughput.
2026-03-06  •  This Week in Startups
Wisdom of the $TAO: the future is decentralized AI
🌐 Bittensor is an open-source protocol enabling a decentralized AI network where users mine the $TAO crypto token by contributing compute to power various AI applications (subnets).
⚙️ Crucible Labs developed a wallet that streamlines the allocation of subnet tokens within the Bittensor ecosystem.
⛏️ Mark Jeffrey, co-founder of the Bittensor Fund Stillcore Capital, demonstrated training an AI agent specifically for mining cryptocurrency.
2026-03-06  •  AI Daily Brief
GPT-5.4 First Test Results
📊 GPT 5.4 demonstrated a 69.2% to 70.8% win rate (or 82% to 83% with ties) against human professionals on the GDP val benchmark across 44 occupations, potentially saving 4 hours and 38 minutes on a 7-hour task.
🎯 GPT 5.4 significantly improved click accuracy, long trajectory reasoning, speed, and memory when navigating complex, 20-year-old hyperdense insurance portals, a key challenge for AI agents.
💰 OpenAI's COO Brad LCAP stated that GPT 5.4 has been specifically enhanced for the finance sector, offering improved financial modeling and analysis, and integrating with Excel and data providers like Factivia and S&P Global.
♦
AI Security
Tue, 03 Mar 2026 19:34:31 GMT  •  IBM
AI Agent Security Best Practices and Tutorial
🤖 AI agents are transitioning from experimental demos to real-time, autonomous systems embedded in everyday workflows.
⚠️ Misconfigured AI agents can leak sensitive data, trigger unauthorized API calls, or expose entire datasets through subtle prompt injection attacks.
🛡️ IBM's BeeAI framework demonstrates applying permissions, role-based access control (RBAC), guardrails, and observability to reduce AI agent security risks.
Tue, 03 Mar 2026 11:09:30 GMT  •  Unit 42
Fooling AI Agents: Web-Based Indirect Prompt Injection Observed in the Wild
🕸️ Indirect prompt injection (IDPI) involves adversaries embedding hidden or manipulated instructions within website content that is later ingested by a Large Language Model (LLM).
🚨 Analysis of large-scale real-world telemetry shows that IDPI is no longer merely theoretical but is actively being weaponized in the wild.
🕵️ Researchers identified 22 distinct techniques attackers used in the wild to put together payloads for web-based indirect prompt injection.
Sat, 07 Mar 2026 14:07:35 GMT  •  StartupHub.ai
OWASP Top 10 LLM Risks Explained
⚠️ IBM Distinguished Engineer Jeff Crume outlines that Large Language Models (LLMs) can be easily manipulated to leak sensitive information or perform unintended actions via crafted prompts, exposed training files, or malicious plugins.
📝 The OWASP community has extended its focus to LLMs, releasing a "Top 10 for LLM Applications" list to guide developers in mitigating common and critical security threats.
🎯 Prompt Injection is the most prevalent threat, where attackers manipulate the LLM by injecting malicious prompts to override system instructions, potentially leading to data leakage or harmful command execution.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 14:30:25 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
What is AI Security? Top Security Risks in LLM Applications
⚙️ AI security refers to the process of protecting AI models, training data, AI applications, and supporting infrastructure from manipulation, unauthorized access, and misuse.
📈 McKinsey’s 2023 Global AI Survey reported that 55% of organizations are using AI in at least one business function.
📉 Research indicates that 45% of AI-generated code contains security vulnerabilities, and prompt injection attacks successfully bypass safeguards in many LLM applications.
2026-03-05
Benjamin Feuer, Lucas Rosenblatt, Oussama Elachqar
Towards Provably Unbiased LLM Judges via Bias-Bounded Evaluation
LLM-as-judge adversarial NIST taxonomy
As AI models progress beyond simple chatbots into more complex workflows, we draw ever closer to the event horizon beyond which AI systems will be utilized in autonomous, self-maintaining feedback loops. Any autonomous AI system will depend on automated, verifiable rewards and feedback; in settings where ground truth is sparse or non-deterministic, one practical source of such rewards is an LLM-as-a-Judge. Although LLM judges continue to improve, the literature has yet to introduce systems capable of enforcing standards with strong guarantees, particularly when bias vectors are unknown or adversarially discovered. To remedy this issue, we propose average bias-boundedness (A-BB), an algorithmic framework which formally guarantees reductions of harm/impact as a result of any measurable bias in an LLM judge. Evaluating on Arena-Hard-Auto with four LLM judges, we achieve (tau=0.5, delta=0.01) bias-bounded guarantees while retaining 61-99% correlation with original rankings across formatting and schematic bias settings, with most judge-bias combinations exceeding 80%. The code to reproduce our findings is available at https://github.com/penfever/bias-bounded-evaluation.
2026-03-05
Guo Chen, Lidong Lu, Yicheng Liu et al.
Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline
agentic AI
While datasets for video understanding have scaled to hour-long durations, they typically consist of densely concatenated clips that differ from natural, unscripted daily life. To bridge this gap, we introduce MM-Lifelong, a dataset designed for Multimodal Lifelong Understanding. Comprising 181.1 hours of footage, it is structured across Day, Week, and Month scales to capture varying temporal densities. Extensive evaluations reveal two critical failure modes in current paradigms: end-to-end MLLMs suffer from a Working Memory Bottleneck due to context saturation, while representative agentic baselines experience Global Localization Collapse when navigating sparse, month-long timelines. To address this, we propose the Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), which employs dynamic memory management to iteratively update a recursive belief state, significantly outperforming existing methods. Finally, we establish dataset splits designed to isolate temporal and domain biases, providing a rigorous foundation for future research in supervised learning and out-of-distribution generalization.
2026-03-05
Artem Vazhentsev (Independent Researcher), Maria Marina, Daniil Moskovskiy et al.
Leveraging LLM Parametric Knowledge for Fact Checking without Retrieval
agentic AI
Trustworthiness is a core research challenge for agentic AI systems built on Large Language Models (LLMs). To enhance trust, natural language claims from diverse sources, including human-written text, web content, and model outputs, are commonly checked for factuality by retrieving external knowledge and using an LLM to verify the faithfulness of claims to the retrieved evidence. As a result, such methods are constrained by retrieval errors and external data availability, while leaving the models intrinsic fact-verification capabilities largely unused. We propose the task of fact-checking without retrieval, focusing on the verification of arbitrary natural language claims, independent of their source. To study this setting, we introduce a comprehensive evaluation framework focused on generalization, testing robustness to (i) long-tail knowledge, (ii) variation in claim sources, (iii) multilinguality, and (iv) long-form generation. Across 9 datasets, 18 methods and 3 models, our experiments indicate that logit-based approaches often underperform compared to those that leverage internal model representations. Building on this finding, we introduce INTRA, a method that exploits interactions between internal representations and achieves state-of-the-art performance with strong generalization. More broadly, our work establishes fact-checking without retrieval as a promising research direction that can complement retrieval-based frameworks, improve scalability, and enable the use of such systems as reward signals during training or as components integrated into the generation process.
2026-03-05
Hugo Buurmeijer, Carmen Amo Alonso, Aiden Swann et al.
Observing and Controlling Features in Vision-Language-Action Models
safety NIST taxonomy
Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) have shown remarkable progress towards embodied intelligence. While their architecture partially resembles that of Large Language Models (LLMs), VLAs exhibit higher complexity due to their multi-modal inputs/outputs and often hybrid nature of transformer and diffusion heads. This is part of the reason why insights from mechanistic interpretability in LLMs, which explain how the internal model representations relate to their output behavior, do not trivially transfer to VLA counterparts. In this work, we propose to close this gap by introducing and analyzing two main concepts: feature-observability and feature-controllability. In particular, we first study features that are linearly encoded in representation space, and show how they can be observed by means of a linear classifier. Then, we use a minimal linear intervention grounded in optimal control to accurately place internal representations and steer the VLA's output towards a desired region. Our results show that targeted, lightweight interventions can reliably steer a robot's behavior while preserving closed-loop capabilities. We demonstrate on different VLA architectures ($π_{0.5}$ and OpenVLA) through simulation experiments that VLAs possess interpretable internal structure amenable to online adaptation without fine-tuning, enabling real-time alignment with user preferences and task requirements.
2026-03-05
Weijie Lyu, Ming-Hsuan Yang, Zhixin Shu
FaceCam: Portrait Video Camera Control via Scale-Aware Conditioning
NIST taxonomy
We introduce FaceCam, a system that generates video under customizable camera trajectories for monocular human portrait video input. Recent camera control approaches based on large video-generation models have shown promising progress but often exhibit geometric distortions and visual artifacts on portrait videos due to scale-ambiguous camera representations or 3D reconstruction errors. To overcome these limitations, we propose a face-tailored scale-aware representation for camera transformations that provides deterministic conditioning without relying on 3D priors. We train a video generation model on both multi-view studio captures and in-the-wild monocular videos, and introduce two camera-control data generation strategies: synthetic camera motion and multi-shot stitching, to exploit stationary training cameras while generalizing to dynamic, continuous camera trajectories at inference time. Experiments on Ava-256 dataset and diverse in-the-wild videos demonstrate that FaceCam achieves superior performance in camera controllability, visual quality, identity and motion preservation.

Microsoft: Anthropic Claude remains available to customers except the Defense Department
🤝 Microsoft confirmed Anthropic Claude models will remain available to its customers, except for the U.S. Department of Defense.
🛡️ The Department of Defense designated Anthropic as a supply chain risk for refusing unrestricted access for applications like mass surveillance.
⚖️ Microsoft's lawyers concluded that Anthropic products, including Claude, can still be used through M365, GitHub, and Microsoft’s AI Foundry for non-defense related projects.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 19:00:22 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
🔎 Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 identified 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox, 14 of which were high-severity, over a two-week period.
🛠️ Most of the bugs discovered by Claude have been resolved in Firefox version 148, released in February.
💸 The team spent $4,000 in API credits attempting to create proof-of-concept exploits, succeeding in only two instances.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 14:28:31 +0000  •  TechCrunch
TriZetto confirms 3.4M people’s health and personal data was stolen during breach
🚨 Health tech giant TriZetto confirmed that over 3.4 million people's personal and health information was stolen in a 2024 cyberattack.
⏱️ TriZetto detected the breach on October 2, 2025, nearly a year after hackers first gained access in November 2024.
🩺 Stolen data includes patient names, dates of birth, home addresses, Social Security numbers, and health insurance details from eligibility transaction reports.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 14:02:52 -0500  •  NPR
Poll: A majority of Americans opposes U.S. military action in Iran
📊 A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows 56% of Americans oppose the current U.S. military action in Iran, which began Feb. 28.
👎 Only 36% of respondents approve of President Trump's handling of Iran, with 55% considering Iran a minor or no threat.
📋 The survey included 1,591 national respondents and was conducted from Monday through Wednesday, following U.S. and Israeli bombings in Iran.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 13:52:39 -0500  •  NPR
Your Winter Paralympics primer: What, who and how to watch
🏟️ The 2026 Winter Paralympic Games will commence Friday in Verona, Italy, marking the 50th anniversary of the event.
🏅 Over 10 days, 665 athletes will compete for a record 79 medals across six sports, including para Alpine skiing and wheelchair curling.
📺 NBC and Peacock will broadcast the opening ceremony live at 2 p.m. ET, with an 8 p.m. primetime re-broadcast.

YouTube

How cosplaying Ancient Rome led to the scientific revolution – Ada Palmer

Tue, 03 Mar 2026 19:34:31 GMT  •  IBM
AI Agent Security Best Practices and Tutorial
🛡️ AI agents are susceptible to prompt injection, over-permissioning, adversarial examples, data poisoning, and model-extraction attacks.
⚙️ IBM's BeeAI framework helps reduce AI agent security risks through permissions, role-based access control (RBAC), guardrails, and observability.
🏛️ Strong security controls for AI agent systems are critical in regulated industries like healthcare, finance, and government for GDPR compliance.
Tue, 03 Mar 2026 11:09:30 GMT  •  Unit 42
Fooling AI Agents: Web-Based Indirect Prompt Injection Observed in the Wild
🚨 Indirect prompt injection (IDPI) is being actively weaponized in the wild, moving beyond theoretical risks.
🔬 Attackers utilize 22 distinct payload engineering techniques for web-based indirect prompt injection.
😈 Observed IDPI attacks include AI-based ad review evasion, SEO manipulation for phishing, data destruction, and unauthorized transactions.
Sat, 28 Feb 2026 17:21:00 GMT  •  The Hacker News
ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
🔓 A high-severity flaw, codenamed ClawJacked, allowed malicious websites to hijack locally running OpenClaw AI agents via WebSocket.
🔑 The ClawJacked flaw exploited OpenClaw's missing rate-limiting mechanism for localhost, allowing brute-forcing of gateway passwords.
🛠️ OpenClaw released version 2026.2.25 on February 26, 2026, to fix the ClawJacked vulnerability, urging users to update promptly.
Fri, 06 Mar 2026 14:30:25 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
What is AI Security? Top Security Risks in LLM Applications
📈 McKinsey’s 2023 Global AI Survey found 55% of organizations use AI in at least one business function.
📉 Research indicates that 45% of AI-generated code contains security vulnerabilities.
⚠️ Prompt injection attacks successfully bypass safeguards in many Large Language Model (LLM) applications.
2026-03-05
Benjamin Feuer, Lucas Rosenblatt, Oussama Elachqar
Towards Provably Unbiased LLM Judges via Bias-Bounded Evaluation
LLM-as-judge adversarial NIST taxonomy
As AI models progress beyond simple chatbots into more complex workflows, we draw ever closer to the event horizon beyond which AI systems will be utilized in autonomous, self-maintaining feedback loops. Any autonomous AI system will depend on automated, verifiable rewards and feedback; in settings where ground truth is sparse or non-deterministic, one practical source of such rewards is an LLM-as-a-Judge. To remedy this issue, we propose average bias-boundedness (A-BB), an algorithmic framework which formally guarantees reductions of harm/impact as a result of any measurable bias in an LLM judge. Evaluating on Arena-Hard-Auto with four LLM judges, we achieve (tau=0.5, delta=0.01) bias-bounded guarantees while retaining 61-99% correlation with original rankings across formatting and schematic bias settings, with most judge-bias combinations exceeding 80%. The code to reproduce our findings is available at https://github.com/penfever/bias-bounded-evaluation.
2026-03-05
Guo Chen, Lidong Lu, Yicheng Liu et al.
Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline
agentic AI
While datasets for video understanding have scaled to hour-long durations, they typically consist of densely concatenated clips that differ from natural, unscripted daily life. To bridge this gap, we introduce MM-Lifelong, a dataset designed for Multimodal Lifelong Understanding. Comprising 181.1 hours of footage, it is structured across Day, Week, and Month scales to capture varying temporal densities. Extensive evaluations reveal two critical failure modes in current paradigms: end-to-end MLLMs suffer from a Working Memory Bottleneck due to context saturation, while representative agentic baselines experience Global Localization Collapse when navigating sparse, month-long timelines. To address this, we propose the Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), which employs dynamic memory management to iteratively update a recursive belief state, significantly outperforming existing methods. Finally, we establish dataset splits designed to isolate temporal and domain biases, providing a rigorous foundation for future research in supervised learning and out-of-distribution generalization.
2026-03-05
Artem Vazhentsev (Independent Researcher), Maria Marina, Daniil Moskovskiy et al.
Leveraging LLM Parametric Knowledge for Fact Checking without Retrieval
agentic AI
Trustworthiness is a core research challenge for agentic AI systems built on Large Language Models (LLMs). To enhance trust, natural language claims from diverse sources, including human-written text, web content, and model outputs, are commonly checked for factuality by retrieving external knowledge and using an LLM to verify the faithfulness of claims to the retrieved evidence. As a result, such methods are constrained by retrieval errors and external data availability, while leaving the models intrinsic fact-verification capabilities largely unused. To study this setting, we introduce a comprehensive evaluation framework focused on generalization, testing robustness to (i) long-tail knowledge, (ii) variation in claim sources, (iii) multilinguality, and (iv) long-form generation. Across 9 datasets, 18 methods and 3 models, our experiments indicate that logit-based approaches often underperform compared to those that leverage internal model representations. Building on this finding, we introduce INTRA, a method that exploits interactions between internal representations and achieves state-of-the-art performance with strong generalization. More broadly, our work establishes fact-checking without retrieval as a promising research direction that can complement retrieval-based frameworks, improve scalability, and enable the use of such systems as reward signals during training or as components integrated into the generation process.
2026-03-05
Hugo Buurmeijer, Carmen Amo Alonso, Aiden Swann et al.
Observing and Controlling Features in Vision-Language-Action Models
safety NIST taxonomy
Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) have shown remarkable progress towards embodied intelligence. While their architecture partially resembles that of Large Language Models (LLMs), VLAs exhibit higher complexity due to their multi-modal inputs/outputs and often hybrid nature of transformer and diffusion heads. This is part of the reason why insights from mechanistic interpretability in LLMs, which explain how the internal model representations relate to their output behavior, do not trivially transfer to VLA counterparts. In this work, we propose to close this gap by introducing and analyzing two main concepts: feature-observability and feature-controllability. In particular, we first study features that are linearly encoded in representation space, and show how they can be observed by means of a linear classifier. Then, we use a minimal linear intervention grounded in optimal control to accurately place internal representations and steer the VLA's output towards a desired region. Our results show that targeted, lightweight interventions can reliably steer a robot's behavior while preserving closed-loop capabilities. We demonstrate on different VLA architectures ($π_{0.5}$ and OpenVLA) through simulation experiments that VLAs possess interpretable internal structure amenable to online adaptation without fine-tuning, enabling real-time alignment with user preferences and task requirements.
2026-03-03
Liu Yang, Zeyu Nie, Andrew Liu et al.
ParEVO: Synthesizing Code for Irregular Data: High-Performance Parallelism through Agentic Evolution
agentic AI
The transition from sequential to parallel computing is essential for modern high-performance applications but is hindered by the steep learning curve of concurrent programming. This challenge is magnified for irregular data structures (such as sparse graphs, unbalanced trees, and non-uniform meshes) where static scheduling fails and data dependencies are unpredictable. Current Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail catastrophically on these tasks, generating code plagued by subtle race conditions, deadlocks, and sub-optimal scaling. We bridge this gap with ParEVO, a framework designed to synthesize high-performance parallel algorithms for irregular data. Our contributions include: (1) The Parlay-Instruct Corpus, a curated dataset of 13,820 tasks synthesized via a "Critic-Refine" pipeline that explicitly filters for empirically performant algorithms that effectively utilize Work-Span parallel primitives; (2) specialized DeepSeek, Qwen, and Gemini models fine-tuned to align probabilistic generation with the rigorous semantics of the ParlayLib library; and (3) an Evolutionary Coding Agent (ECA) that improves the "last mile" of correctness by iteratively repairing code using feedback from compilers, dynamic race detectors, and performance profilers. On the ParEval benchmark, ParEVO achieves an average 106x speedup (with a maximum of 1103x) across the suite, and a robust 13.6x speedup specifically on complex irregular graph problems, outperforming state-of-the-art commercial models. Furthermore, our evolutionary approach matches state-of-the-art expert human baselines, achieving up to a 4.1x speedup on specific highly-irregular kernels. Source code and datasets are available at https://github.com/WildAlg/ParEVO.

Top News
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 18:06:55 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Google says half of all zero-days it tracked in 2025 targeted buggy enterprise tech
📊 In 2025, 48% of zero-day vulnerabilities tracked by Google targeted enterprise devices, marking a new high for hackers.
🛡️ About half of those enterprise zero-days exploited security and networking devices from vendors like Cisco, Fortinet, Ivanti, and VMware.
📉 The remaining 52% of zero-day bugs in 2025 were found in consumer products, particularly operating systems and mobile devices from Microsoft, Google, and Apple.
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 18:00:15 +0000  •  TechCrunch
OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
🧠 OpenAI released GPT-5.4, available in a standard version, GPT-5.4 Thinking for reasoning, and GPT-5.4 Pro for high performance.
🚀 GPT-5.4 achieved a record 83% on OpenAI’s GDPval test for knowledge work tasks and set new records in OSWorld-Verified and WebArena Verified benchmarks.
🔒 The new GPT-5.4 model is 33% less likely to make errors in individual claims and 18% less likely to have overall response errors compared to GPT 5.2.
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 16:50:20 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Meta sued over AI smart glasses’ privacy concerns, after workers reviewed nudity, sex, and other footage
⚖️ Meta faces a U.S. lawsuit filed by Gina Bartone and Mateo Canu alleging privacy law violations and false advertising regarding its AI smart glasses.
🕵️‍♀️ An investigation found workers at a Kenya-based subcontractor reviewed sensitive customer footage, including nudity and sexual acts, from Meta AI smart glasses.
📈 Over seven million Meta smart glasses were sold in 2025, and users cannot opt out of their footage being fed into a data pipeline for review.
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 14:20:38 -0500  •  NPR
Trump fires Kristi Noem as DHS chief, names Sen. Markwayne Mullin to replace her
🏛️ President Trump fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and named Oklahoma GOP Sen. Markwayne Mullin as her replacement.
✈️ Kristi Noem will transition to a new role as Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas, a security initiative announced on Saturday in Doral, Florida.
🚧 Noem faced bipartisan criticism during congressional hearings over her leadership, including an immigration enforcement surge in Minneapolis that resulted in two U.S. citizen deaths.
Thu, 05 Mar 2026 14:19:50 -0500  •  NPR
Can a digital tablet cut back a country's overuse of antibiotics?
💊 A study across 32 rural Rwandan clinics revealed that 71% of pediatric visits ended with an antibiotic prescription, indicating significant overuse.
📈 Researchers developed ePOCT+, a tablet-based tool, which successfully reduced antibiotic prescription rates from 71% to 25% in the same 32 clinics.
✅ The implementation of the ePOCT+ tool did not compromise health outcomes for children, according to the findings reported in PLOS Medicine.
♦
YouTube
2026-03-05  •  AI Daily Brief
Is OpenAI the New GitHub?
🤔 The AI industry is debating whether recent job changes represent an AI-driven headcount reset or a correction of prior COVID overhiring.
🚀 Google has released its new product, Nano Banana 2.
📈 Claude's user signups are experiencing a surge, demonstrating strong growth in user adoption.
2026-03-05  •  AI Daily Brief
The Big Questions Shaping the Consumer AI Battle
☁️ OpenAI is reportedly developing a rival to GitHub, indicating expansion into code hosting and collaboration.
🛠️ Meta is reorganizing its AI teams to better compete in the evolving artificial intelligence landscape.
🛍️ Amazon is exploring the integration of advertisements within its AI chatbots, suggesting new monetization strategies for conversational AI.
2026-03-05  •  Morning Brew Daily
Apple’s low-cost laptop could win over students
💥 NATO entered the conflict with Iran for the first time, shooting down a missile headed toward Turkey.
🛡️ The Android-only "Nearby Glasses" app detects Bluetooth signals from smart glasses within 10 to 50 feet, alerting users to potential surveillance.
✈️ American Express launched "Sidecar," a 1,500-square-foot mini lounge in Las Vegas with 33 seats, accessible 90 minutes before departure.
2026-03-05  •  Theo - t3.gg
The drama never ends...
🚨 OpenAI's contract with the Department of War includes a clause prohibiting intentional use for domestic surveillance of US persons and nationals and excludes intelligence agencies like the NSA.
⚖️ Congress is preparing legislative responses to address concerns about the Pentagon's use of AI, including a potential amendment to the Defense Production Act to protect vendors.
🧠 Consider that the government's stance in AI deals may stem from a desire to avoid restrictive policies on purchased tech, rather than solely seeking specific malicious uses.
2026-03-05  •  AI Daily Brief
White Hot Cursor Doubles Revenue
💰 Cursor surpassed $2 billion in annual recurring revenue (ARR) in February, doubling its revenue in just three months, driven largely by enterprise customers.
📉 ChatGPT uninstalls tripled in a single day and one-star reviews surged by 775% following the DoD controversy, while Claude app downloads increased by 37% on Friday and 51% on Saturday.
🛡️ Sam Altman confirmed revisions to OpenAI's Pentagon contract, adding language that prohibits intentional use of AI systems for domestic surveillance of US persons and nationals, and excludes intelligence agencies like the NSA.
2026-03-05  •  This Week in Startups
Is Anthropic Making the Biggest Mistake in AI History? | E2258
📈 Anthropic is projected to generate nearly $20 billion in annual revenue, more than doubling its run rate from $9 billion at the end of 2025.
💸 Learn from the "toxic wealth" lesson: the CEO of Eagle Computer died in an auto crash hours after his company's IPO, leading to keyman clauses in VC deals that restrict high-risk activities.
📚 Utilize Notebook LM's new video overview feature to create immersive video presentations from your documents, enhancing learning and content creation.
♦
AI Security
Tue, 03 Mar 2026 19:34:31 GMT  •  IBM
AI Agent Security Best Practices and Tutorial
🛡️ pt injection attacks.
🔍 This AI agent security best practices guide covers authentication, access controls, data safeguards and secure multi‑agent automation.
⚠️ As AI models increasingly automate decision‑making workflows, the need for anomaly detection and risk‑mitigating controls becomes critical.
Sat, 28 Feb 2026 17:21:00 GMT  •  The Hacker News
ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
🛡️ company.
🔍 The attack assumes the following threat model: A developer has OpenClaw set up and running on their laptop, with its gateway, a local WebSocket server, bound to localhost and protected by a password.
⚠️ The attack kicks in when the developer lands on an attacker-controlled website through social engineering or some other means.
Mon, 02 Mar 2026 08:00:00 GMT  •  AIMultiple
10 Risks of Generative AI & How to Mitigate Them
🛡️ question or to create a response to a certain prompt, these models interpret the prompt and induce a response based on their training data.
🔍 Although their training data sets consist of billions of parameters, they are finite pools and the generative models may hallucinate responses from time to time.
⚠️ There can be many potential accuracy risks caused by generative AI models: - Generalization over specificity: Since generative models are designed to generalize across the data they’re trained on, they may not always produce accurate information for specific, nuanced, or out-of-sample queries.
Fri, 27 Feb 2026 15:00:24 GMT  •  wiz.io
What is AI Security? Securing Models, Agents & Data
🛡️ inventory and guardrails: map every AI service and integration, set baseline configs, then monitor for drift and suspicious access patterns.
🔍 How Wiz helps: Wiz AI-SPM brings AI services, identities, and data into one risk view so teams can find the few AI issues that can actually lead to impact, then route fixes to the right owners.
⚠️ What is AI security? AI security is the practice of defending systems using AI-powered capabilities while simultaneously protecting AI assets from emerging threats.
2026-03-04
Furkan Mumcu, Yasin Yilmaz
Robustness of Agentic AI Systems via Adversarially-Aligned Jacobian Regularization
agentic AI adversarial
As Large Language Models (LLMs) transition into autonomous multi-agent ecosystems, robust minimax training becomes essential yet remains prone to instability when highly non-linear policies induce extreme local curvature in the inner maximization. Standard remedies that enforce global Jacobian bounds are overly conservative, suppressing sensitivity in all directions and inducing a large Price of Robustness. We introduce Adversarially-Aligned Jacobian Regularization (AAJR), a trajectory-aligned approach that controls sensitivity strictly along adversarial ascent directions. We prove that AAJR yields a strictly larger admissible policy class than global constraints under mild conditions, implying a weakly smaller approximation gap and reduced nominal performance degradation. Furthermore, we derive step-size conditions under which AAJR controls effective smoothness along optimization trajectories and ensures inner-loop stability. These results provide a structural theory for agentic robustness that decouples minimax stability from global expressivity restrictions.
2026-03-04
Boyuan (Keven), Guan, Wencong Cui et al.
A Dual-Helix Governance Approach Towards Reliable Agentic AI for WebGIS Development
agentic AI
WebGIS development requires rigor, yet agentic AI frequently fails due to five large language model (LLM) limitations: context constraints, cross-session forgetting, stochasticity, instruction failure, and adaptation rigidity. We propose a dual-helix governance framework reframing these challenges as structural governance problems that model capacity alone cannot resolve. We implement the framework as a 3-track architecture (Knowledge, Behavior, Skills) that uses a knowledge graph substrate to stabilize execution by externalizing domain facts and enforcing executable protocols, complemented by a self-learning cycle for autonomous knowledge growth. Applying this to the FutureShorelines WebGIS tool, a governed agent refactored a 2,265-line monolithic codebase into modular ES6 components. Results demonstrated a 51\% reduction in cyclomatic complexity and a 7-point increase in maintainability index. A comparative experiment against a zero-shot LLM confirms that externalized governance, not just model capability, drives operational reliability in geospatial engineering. This approach is implemented in the open-source AgentLoom governance toolkit.
2026-03-04
Geraldin Nanfack, Eugene Belilovsky, Elvis Dohmatob
Efficient Refusal Ablation in LLM through Optimal Transport
jailbreak safety
Safety-aligned language models refuse harmful requests through learned refusal behaviors encoded in their internal representations. Recent activation-based jailbreaking methods circumvent these safety mechanisms by applying orthogonal projections to remove refusal directions, but these approaches treat refusal as a one-dimensional phenomenon and ignore the rich distributional structure of model activations. We introduce a principled framework based on optimal transport theory that transforms the entire distribution of harmful activations to match harmless ones. By combining PCA with closed-form Gaussian optimal transport, we achieve efficient computation in high-dimensional representation spaces while preserving essential geometric structure. Across six models (Llama-2, Llama-3.1, Qwen-2.5; 7B-32B parameters), our method achieves up to 11% higher attack success rates than state-of-the-art baselines while maintaining comparable perplexity, demonstrating superior preservation of model capabilities. Critically, we discover that layer-selective intervention (applying optimal transport to 1-2 carefully chosen layers at approximately 40-60% network depth) substantially outperforms full-network interventions, revealing that refusal mechanisms may be localized rather than distributed. Our analysis provides new insights into the geometric structure of safety representations and suggests that current alignment methods may be vulnerable to distributional attacks beyond simple direction removal.
2026-03-04
Haoyu Liu, Dingcheng Li, Lukas Rutishauser et al.
Dual-Modality Multi-Stage Adversarial Safety Training: Robustifying Multimodal Web Agents Against Cross-Modal Attacks
adversarial safety
Multimodal web agents that process both screenshots and accessibility trees are increasingly deployed to interact with web interfaces, yet their dual-stream architecture opens an underexplored attack surface: an adversary who injects content into the webpage DOM simultaneously corrupts both observation channels with a consistent deceptive narrative. Our vulnerability analysis on MiniWob++ reveals that attacks including a visual component far outperform text-only injections, exposing critical gaps in text-centric VLM safety training. Motivated by this finding, we propose Dual-Modality Multi-Stage Adversarial Safety Training (DMAST), a framework that formalizes the agent-attacker interaction as a two-player zero-sum Markov game and co-trains both players through a three-stage pipeline: (1) imitation learning from a strong teacher model, (2) oracle-guided supervised fine-tuning that uses a novel zero-acknowledgment strategy to instill task-focused reasoning under adversarial noise, and (3) adversarial reinforcement learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) self-play. On out-of-distribution tasks, DMAST substantially mitigates adversarial risks while simultaneously doubling task completion efficiency. Our approach significantly outperforms established training-based and prompt-based defenses, demonstrating genuine co-evolutionary progress and robust generalization to complex, unseen environments.
2026-03-03
Aradhye Agarwal, Gurdit Siyan, Yash Pandya et al.
Learning When to Act or Refuse: Guarding Agentic Reasoning Models for Safe Multi-Step Tool Use
prompt injection agentic AI privacy adversarial safety
Agentic language models operate in a fundamentally different safety regime than chat models: they must plan, call tools, and execute long-horizon actions where a single misstep, such as accessing files or entering credentials, can cause irreversible harm. Existing alignment methods, largely optimized for static generation and task completion, break down in these settings due to sequential decision-making, adversarial tool feedback, and overconfident intermediate reasoning. We introduce MOSAIC, a post-training framework that aligns agents for safe multi-step tool use by making safety decisions explicit and learnable. MOSAIC structures inference as a plan, check, then act or refuse loop, with explicit safety reasoning and refusal as first-class actions. To train without trajectory-level labels, we use preference-based reinforcement learning with pairwise trajectory comparisons, which captures safety distinctions often missed by scalar rewards. We evaluate MOSAIC zero-shot across three model families, Qwen2.5-7B, Qwen3-4B-Thinking, and Phi-4, and across out-of-distribution benchmarks spanning harmful tasks, prompt injection, benign tool use, and cross-domain privacy leakage. MOSAIC reduces harmful behavior by up to 50%, increases harmful-task refusal by over 20% on injection attacks, cuts privacy leakage, and preserves or improves benign task performance, demonstrating robust generalization across models, domains, and agentic settings.
Generated automatically · Subscribe via RSS

Top News
Wed, 04 Mar 2026 18:50:58 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Google’s Gemini rolls out Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
🗺️ Google has expanded Canvas in AI Mode to all U.S. users in English, following its initial launch as a Google Labs experiment last year.
🎨 The feature generates code for shareable apps or games from user ideas and transforms research reports into web pages, quizzes, or audio overviews.
🔍 Users access Canvas via a tool menu (+) in AI Mode and can integrate information from the web and Google’s Knowledge Graph for projects.
Wed, 04 Mar 2026 17:32:03 GMT  •  The Washington Post
Anthropic’s AI tool Claude central to U.S. campaign in Iran, amid a bitter feud
🇺🇸 Anthropic’s AI tool, Claude, is central to the ongoing U.S. campaign in Iran.
⚔️ The deployment of Claude occurs amid a bitter feud surrounding the AI company.
🇮🇷 Claude’s specific role in the U.S. military operations against Iran is a key point of ongoing discussion.
Wed, 04 Mar 2026 17:20:01 +0000  •  TechCrunch
The US military is still using Claude — but defense-tech clients are fleeing
🎯 Anthropic’s Claude AI is actively used for targeting decisions in the ongoing U.S. aerial attack on Iran.
🛰️ Claude, paired with Palantir’s Maven system, suggested hundreds of targets, issued precise coordinates, and prioritized them for strikes in Tehran.
🚪 Lockheed Martin and at least 10 portfolio companies from J2 Ventures have already replaced Claude models for their defense use cases this week.
Wed, 04 Mar 2026 13:53:01 -0500  •  NPR
Colossal Biosciences breeds controversy while trying to revive mammoths
🐘 Colossal Biosciences aims to resurrect extinct species such as the woolly mammoth, Tasmanian tiger, and dodo bird.
🔬 The company opened its new 55,000-square-foot lab in Dallas, employing 260 geneticists, reproductive biologists, and ecologists.
🧬 Chief Science Officer Beth Shapiro demonstrated extracting DNA from a 2-foot mammoth tusk to compare with living elephant genetic material.
Wed, 04 Mar 2026 13:10:36 -0500  •  NPR
GOP Rep. Tony Gonzales heads to a runoff in Texas amid a new ethics probe in the House
🗳️ Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas) advanced to a May 26 Republican primary runoff against challenger Brandon Herrera.
⚖️ The House Ethics Committee launched a formal probe into allegations of sexual misconduct or unfair discrimination towards a congressional office employee.
✉️ The investigation follows published explicit text messages between Gonzales and his former employee Regina Santos-Aviles, who died by suicide last September.
♦
YouTube
2026-03-04  •  This Week in Startups
Why Venice AI was the “Go-To” for OpenClaw
🛠️ OpenClaw selected Venice AI as its primary development partner for building sophisticated AI agents.
📈 Venice AI facilitated rapid scaling and efficient deployment of OpenClaw's autonomous AI systems.
🌟 Venice AI secured its "go-to" status by offering specialized AI infrastructure tailored for emergent agentic startups like OpenClaw.
2026-03-04  •  AI Daily Brief
The Rise of the Zero Human Company
🤖 The cost of execution for new businesses is dramatically falling due to the rise of autonomous, zero-human AI startups.
💰 Cursor, an AI company, achieved $2 billion in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) after doubling its revenue in just three months.
⚠️ Recent outages for Claude AI indicate a significant surge in demand for its services.
2026-03-04  •  Morning Brew Daily
Gas prices spike the most in 4 years
⏰ British Columbia is adopting year-round Daylight Saving Time, citing 93% public support from a 2019 report to reduce disruptions.
⚽ The upcoming World Cup, hosted by the US, Mexico, and Canada, is projected to attract 6 billion viewers, three-quarters of the world's population.
🎻 Lufthansa changed its carry-on policy for musical instruments after concert violinist Carolyn Whitman's 1782 Italian violin was almost denied on a flight from Helsinki.
2026-03-04  •  Theo - t3.gg
I can't believe nobody's done this before...
⚡ OpenAI is transitioning its APIs from REST calls to WebSockets, a significant shift for developers using their models.
🚀 Implement OpenAI's new WebSocket APIs to reduce bandwidth by over 90% and improve speed by 20-30% for agentic runs.
⚙️ Utilize WebSocket's persistent connection to avoid re-sending full context on every turn, speeding up agentic runs with 20+ tool calls by 20-40%.
2026-03-04  •  Matt Wolfe
This Free App Runs AI Offline On Your iPhone
📱 Download the free "Locally AI" app to run open-weight AI models like Qwen 3.5 directly on your iPhone without an internet connection.
🔒 Use Locally AI to ensure privacy, as all prompts and data remain on your device and are not sent to cloud services like OpenAI or Google.
🧠 Leverage Locally AI's "thinking mode" for chain-of-thought processing on device, allowing models like the Qwen 3.5 2B parameter to generate brainstorming ideas quickly and offline.
2026-03-03  •  Morning Brew Daily
Why sweetgreen is becoming millennial cringe
🎬 Pixar's new film "Hoppers" achieved a 97% Rotten Tomatoes score, marking its highest-rated movie in a decade ahead of its Friday release.
📺 Paramount Plus and HBO Max are set to merge into a single streaming platform under David Ellison, aiming for over 200 million subscribers and $69 billion in revenue.
🏁 Michael Jordan is finding success as a NASCAR team owner, a role where he provides funding and sponsorship without direct team management, unlike his NBA executive positions.
2026-03-03  •  Jeff Su
The Top 1% Build AI Presentations Differently. Here's How.
💡 Combine AI tools: Use LLMs like ChatGPT for analytically sharp text content and AI presentation tools like Gamma for polished visuals in your presentations.
✍️ When drafting presentations in Gamma, use three dashes (---) to delineate separate slides in your text outline and use the "preserve this exact text" option to maintain strong custom titles.
📊 Streamline presentation updates by using Gamma's built-in AI agent to search the web for current data, automatically update statistics, and convert text-heavy slides into visual charts like waterfall charts.
2026-03-03  •  Theo - t3.gg
I'm so f***ing tired of Obsidian.
❌ Do not consider Obsidian as a viable replacement for Notion if your workflow involves team collaboration or complex system management beyond simple markdown files.
📱 Be aware that Obsidian Sync functionality is limited, often struggling between individual devices and largely failing to support multi-user environments for shared projects.
💡 Understand that Notion's value for complex operations, like managing video production (topics, research, sponsors), lies in its infrastructure and document relationships, which file-based systems like Obsidian cannot easily replicate.
♦
AI Security
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 23:00:43 GMT  •  TechTarget
LLM firewalls emerge as a new AI security layer
🛡️ risks.
🔍 Among the most visible of these emerging technologies are so-called LLM firewalls.
⚠️ What's an LLM firewall? With the coupling of AI and operational systems come the risks of prompt injection attacks, model poisoning, data leaks and dangerous misconfigurations.
Tue, 03 Mar 2026 19:34:31 GMT  •  IBM
AI Agent Security Best Practices and Tutorial
🛡️ pt injection attacks.
🔍 This AI agent security best practices guide covers authentication, access controls, data safeguards and secure multi‑agent automation.
⚠️ As AI models increasingly automate decision‑making workflows, the need for anomaly detection and risk‑mitigating controls becomes critical.

Fooling AI Agents: Web-Based Indirect Prompt Injection Observed in the Wild
🛡️ AI agents are becoming deeply integrated into web browsers, search engines and automated content-processing pipelines.
🔍 While these integrations can expand functionality, they also introduce a new and largely underexplored attack surface.
⚠️ One particularly concerning class of threats is indirect prompt injection (IDPI), in which adversaries embed hidden or manipulated instructions within website content that is later ingested by an LLM.
Fri, 27 Feb 2026 15:00:24 GMT  •  wiz.io
What is AI Security? Securing Models, Agents & Data
🛡️ inventory and guardrails: map every AI service and integration, set baseline configs, then monitor for drift and suspicious access patterns.
🔍 How Wiz helps: Wiz AI-SPM brings AI services, identities, and data into one risk view so teams can find the few AI issues that can actually lead to impact, then route fixes to the right owners.
⚠️ What is AI security? AI security is the practice of defending systems using AI-powered capabilities while simultaneously protecting AI assets from emerging threats.
2026-03-03  •  Cullen Anderson, Narmeen Oozeer, Foad Namjoo et al.
Understanding and Mitigating Dataset Corruption in LLM Steering
adversarial safety
🛡️ Contrastive steering has been shown as a simple and effective method to adjust the generative behavior of LLMs at inference time.
🔍 It uses examples of prompt responses with and without a trait to identify a direction in an intermediate activation layer, and then shifts activations in this 1-dimensional subspace.
⚠️ However, despite its growing use in AI safety applications, the robustness of contrastive steering to noisy or adversarial data corruption is poorly understood.
2026-03-03  •  Aradhye Agarwal, Gurdit Siyan, Yash Pandya et al.
Learning When to Act or Refuse: Guarding Agentic Reasoning Models for Safe Multi-Step Tool Use
agentic AI adversarial safety
🛡️ Agentic language models operate in a fundamentally different safety regime than chat models: they must plan, call tools, and execute long-horizon actions where a single misstep, such as accessing files or entering credentials, can cause irreversible harm.
🔍 Existing alignment methods, largely optimized for static generation and task completion, break down in these settings due to sequential decision-making, adversarial tool feedback, and overconfident intermediate reasoning.
⚠️ We introduce MOSAIC, a.
2026-03-03  •  Achyutha Menon, Magnus Saebo, Tyler Crosse et al.
Inherited Goal Drift: Contextual Pressure Can Undermine Agentic Goals
agentic AI
🛡️ The accelerating adoption of language models (LMs) as agents for deployment in long-context tasks motivates a thorough understanding of goal drift: agents' tendency to deviate from an original objective.
🔍 While prior-generation language model agents have been shown to be susceptible to drift, the extent to which drift affects more recent models remains unclear.
⚠️ In this work, we provide an updated characterization of the extent and causes of goal drift.
2026-03-03  •  Zihang Zeng, Jiaquan Zhang, Pengze Li et al.
AI-for-Science Low-code Platform with Bayesian Adversarial Multi-Agent Framework
adversarial
🛡️ Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate potentials for automating scientific code generation but face challenges in reliability, error propagation in multi-agent workflows, and evaluation in domains with ill-defined success metrics.
🔍 We present a Bayesian adversarial multi-agent framework specifically designed for AI for Science (AI4S) tasks in the form of a Low-code Platform (LCP).
⚠️ Three LLM-based agents are coordinated under the Bayesian framework: a Task Manager that structures user inputs in.
2026-03-03  •  Dadi Guo, Yuejin Xie, Qingyu Liu et al.
Code2Math: Can Your Code Agent Effectively Evolve Math Problems Through Exploration?
agentic AI
🛡️ As large language models (LLMs) advance their mathematical capabilities toward the IMO level, the scarcity of challenging, high-quality problems for training and evaluation has become a significant bottleneck.
🔍 Simultaneously, recent code agents have demonstrated sophisticated skills in agentic coding and reasoning, suggesting that code execution can serve as a scalable environment for mathematical experimentation.
⚠️ In this paper, we investigate the potential of code agents to autonomously evolve.

Mon, 02 Mar 2026 17:18:34 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Tech workers urge DOD, Congress to withdraw Anthropic label as a supply-chain risk
📰 DOD and Anthropic after the AI lab last week refused to give the military unrestricted access to its AI systems.
📢 Anthropic’s two red lines in its negotiations with the Pentagon were that it didn’t want its technology to be used for mass surveillance of Americans or to power autonomous weapons that could target and fire without a human in the loop.
🔍 The DOD said it had no plans to do either of those things but that it didn’t believe it should be limited by the rules of a vendor.
2026-03-02T17:29:46-05:00  •  The Verge
Anthropic upgrades Claude’s memory to attract AI switchers
📰 aude the context and history their previous chatbot already knows.
📢 Anthropic upgrades Claude’s memory to attract AI switchers Claude’s memory feature has a new prompt and importing tool for copying users’ data from other AI platforms.
🔍 Claude’s memory feature has a new prompt and importing tool for copying users’ data from other AI platforms.
2026-03-02T15:22:33-05:00  •  The Verge

Tech workers urge DOD, Congress to withdraw Anthropic label as a supply-chain risk
📰 DOD and Anthropic after the AI lab last week refused to give the military unrestricted access to its AI systems.
📢 Anthropic’s two red lines in its negotiations with the Pentagon were that it didn’t want its technology to be used for mass surveillance of Americans or to power autonomous weapons that could target and fire without a human in the loop.
🔍 The DOD said it had no plans to do either of those things but that it didn’t believe it should be limited by the rules of a vendor.

Apple might use Google servers to store data for its upgraded AI Siri
📰 Apple has asked Google to look into “setting up servers” for a new version of Siri that’s Gemini-powered and meets Apple’s privacy requirements, The Information reports.
📢 Apple had already announced in January that Google’s Gemini AI models would help power the upgraded version of Siri it delayed last year, but The Information’s report indicates Apple might lean even more on Google so it can catch up in AI.
🔍 Apple might use Google servers to store data for its upgraded AI Siri Apple may be leaning even more on Google as it tries to catch up in AI.

The AI Industry Will Hit Trillions by 2030 - Dario Amodei
📈 Expect the AI industry to generate trillions of dollars in revenue before 2030, driven by exponential growth and diffusion.

There is an insurance angle to what's happening in the Strait of Hormuz
🚢 The Strait of Hormuz, a critical global maritime choke point, has seen commercial ship traffic essentially halt due to increased conflict risk.
⛽ One out of every five barrels of the world's oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, making its closure a potential trigger for global crude price spikes and inflation.

👨‍💻 Consider adopting Andrej Karpathy's programming approach: actively architect your code while leveraging LLM autocomplete for repetitive patterns and boilerplate.
⚙️ Utilize AI agents for highly repetitive, boilerplate coding tasks or patterns frequently found in internet codebases, where they perform best.

2026-03-01  •  Matt Wolfe

🚨 Be aware of OpenAI's "Code Red" and the intensified competition from models like Google Gemini 3 and Nano Banana Pro for market dominance.

🎨 Explore new creative AI tools for video generation, noting Runway Gen 4.5's preference dominance, and be mindful of the rise of "AI slop" in generative content.
2026-03-01  •  Theo - t3.gg
Cursor, Claude Code and Codex all have a BIG problem
🏗️ Prioritize optimizing your codebase for ease, clarity, and speed from the start, ensuring small changes affect few files to maintain long-term quality and developer experience.
💡 Consider using AI coding tools like Cursor, Claude Code, or Codex primarily for quickly vetting ideas and generating initial drafts, but meticulously craft the final production code yourself.
🧹 Avoid merging "cheap code" directly from AI tools; instead, treat AI outputs as exploratory and commit to a philosophy of "file twice, merge once" to prevent future codebase degradation.

Rick Beato: Greatest Guitarists of All Time, History & Future of Music | Lex Fridman Podcast #492
🎸 To learn guitar, start with iconic solos like Jimi Hendrix's "Hey Joe" to grasp foundational scales such as the E minor pentatonic.
🧠 If you're a jazz improviser, prioritize experimentation and novelty before age 30, as that period is often when musicians produce their most innovative work.
🤝 Cultivate and maintain strong friendships and family connections, as these relationships are paramount for a fulfilling life, according to Beato.
♦
AI Security
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 23:00:43 GMT  •  TechTarget
LLM firewalls emerge as a new AI security layer
🛡️ risks.
🔍 Among the most visible of these emerging technologies are so-called LLM firewalls.
⚠️ What's an LLM firewall? With the coupling of AI and operational systems come the risks of prompt injection attacks, model poisoning, data leaks and dangerous misconfigurations.
Tue, 24 Feb 2026 09:04:17 GMT  •  Qualys
DeepSeek Jailbreak Vulnerability Analysis | Qualys TotalAI | Qualys
🛡️ alternative to traditional large-scale AI development, making advanced capabilities more widely available.
🔍 To enhance efficiency while preserving model efficacy, DeepSeek has released multiple distilled versions tailored for different use cases.
⚠️ These variations, built on Llama and Qwen as base models, come in multiple size variants, ranging from smaller, lightweight models suitable for efficiency-focused applications to larger, more powerful versions designed for complex reasoning tasks.
Fri, 27 Feb 2026 15:00:24 GMT  •  wiz.io
What is AI Security? Securing Models, Agents & Data
🛡️ inventory and guardrails: map every AI service and integration, set baseline configs, then monitor for drift and suspicious access patterns.
🔍 How Wiz helps: Wiz AI-SPM brings AI services, identities, and data into one risk view so teams can find the few AI issues that can actually lead to impact, then route fixes to the right owners.
⚠️ What is AI security? AI security is the practice of defending systems using AI-powered capabilities while simultaneously protecting AI assets from emerging threats.
Fri, 27 Feb 2026 10:07:58 GMT  •  csoonline.com
How to make LLMs a defensive advantage without creating a new attack surface
🛡️ summarize an incident in seconds can also generate a believable pretext for a spear phish.
🔍 The same assistant that can draft detection logic can also be induced to leak sensitive context if it is wired into internal knowledge bases without guardrails.
⚠️ I treat LLMs as another high-impact system: define outcomes, model threats and build controls that assume the model will be wrong or manipulated.
2026-02-27  •  Weinan Dai, Hanlin Wu, Qiying Yu et al.
CUDA Agent: Large-Scale Agentic RL for High-Performance CUDA Kernel Generation
agentic AI
🛡️ GPU kernel optimization is fundamental to modern deep learning but remains a highly specialized task requiring deep hardware expertise.
🔍 Despite strong performance in general programming, large language models (LLMs) remain uncompetitive with compiler-based systems such as torch.
⚠️ compile for CUDA kernel generation.
2026-02-27  •  Borja Requena Pozo, Austin Letson, Krystian Nowakowski et al.
A Minimal Agent for Automated Theorem Proving
agentic AI
🛡️ We propose a minimal agentic baseline that enables systematic comparison across different AI-based theorem prover architectures.
🔍 This design implements the core features shared among state-of-the-art systems: iterative proof refinement, library search and context management.
⚠️ We evaluate our baseline using qualitatively different benchmarks and compare various popular models and design choices, and demonstrate competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches, while using a signific.
2026-02-27  •  Jialiang Fan, Weizhe Xu, Mengyu Liu et al.
SafeGen-LLM: Enhancing Safety Generalization in Task Planning for Robotic Systems
safety
🛡️ Safety-critical task planning in robotic systems remains challenging: classical planners suffer from poor scalability, Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based methods generalize poorly, and base Large Language Models (LLMs) cannot guarantee safety.
🔍 To address this gap, we propose safety-generalizable large language models, named SafeGen-LLM.
⚠️ SafeGen-LLM can not only enhance the safety satisfaction of task plans but also generalize well to novel safety properties in various domains.
2026-02-27  •  Boram Jung, Yuliang Li, Hung-Wei Tseng
NSHEDB: Noise-Sensitive Homomorphic Encrypted Database Query Engine
privacy
🛡️ Homomorphic encryption (HE) enables computations directly on encrypted data, offering strong cryptographic guarantees for secure and privacy-preserving data storage and query execution.
🔍 However, despite its theoretical power, practical adoption of HE in database systems remains limited due to extreme cipher-text expansion, memory overhead, and the computational cost of bootstrapping, which resets noise levels for correctness.
⚠️ This paper presents NSHEDB, a secure query processing engine designed.

Column | Asking Eric: Grandson has no motivation; daughter enables him

Podcast Episodes
2026-03-01  •  Lex Fridman Podcast
#492 – Rick Beato: Greatest Guitarists of All Time, History & Future of Music
🎯 #492 – Rick Beato: Greatest Guitarists of All Time, History & Future of Music
2026-02-28  •  This Week in Startups
The Biggest Private Funding Round in History | E2256
💰 OpenAI reportedly raised $110 billion in private funding, with investments from Microsoft, Nvidia, and SoftBank.
📈 ChatGPT currently serves 900 million weekly active users and has 50 million paying subscribers.
🧠 Host Jason Calacanis claimed that AGI has already been achieved, though it is not yet fully implemented.
2026-02-28  •  AI Daily Brief
Who Controls AI?
💬 Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly stated concerns about using AI models for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.
🚨 President Trump posted on Truth Social directing federal agencies to cease using Anthropic products.
🤝 OpenAI subsequently announced its own agreement with the Department of War, claiming similar safeguards were included.
2026-02-28  •  AI Daily Brief
Are 40% Staff Cuts the New AI Normal?
🍌 Google released a new model named Nano Banana 2.

Netflix Walks Away From WBD Deal & Why is Everyone Leaving America?
🎬 Netflix walked away from a potential deal to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery after Paramount made a counter offer.
✂️ Jack Dorsey's company Block cut nearly half of its staff, attributing the reductions to AI's impact.
🏪 Papa John's is closing hundreds of stores, while Waymo is expanding its autonomous driving service into more cities.
♦
AI Security Papers
2026-02-26  •  Kunihiro Miyazaki, Takanobu Kawahara, Stephen Roberts et al.
Toward Expert Investment Teams:A Multi-Agent LLM System with Fine-Grained Trading Tasks
💡 The paper proposes a multi-agent LLM trading framework that explicitly decomposes investment analysis into fine-grained tasks.
📉 Mainstream multi-agent systems often use abstract instructions, leading to degraded inference performance and less transparent decision-making.
📈 The framework aims to improve upon existing systems mimicking analyst and manager roles by detailing real-world workflows.
2026-02-26  •  Sahal Shaji Mullappilly, Mohammed Irfan Kurpath, Omair Mohamed et al.
MediX-R1: Open Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
🔬 MediX-R1 is introduced as an open-ended Reinforcement Learning framework for medical multimodal large language models (MLLMs).
💬 The framework enables clinically grounded, free-form answers in MLLMs, moving beyond conventional multiple-choice formats.
⚙️ MediX-R1 fine-tunes a baseline vision-language backbone with Group Based RL and a composite reward tailored for medical reasoning.
2026-02-26  •  Salim Fares
Utilizing LLMs for Industrial Process Automation
🖥️ Most current publications on LLMs in software engineering focus on widely-used general-purpose programming languages like Python.
🏭 The utility of LLMs for software in industrial process automation, which uses highly specialized proprietary languages, remains underexplored.
🎯 This research aims to assess the application of LLMs within proprietary industrial process automation domains.
2026-02-23  •  Nicole Cho, William Watson, Alec Koppel et al.
No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
🚫 QueryBandits is introduced as a model-agnostic contextual bandit framework designed to adaptively learn online methods for hallucination mitigation in LLMs.
🔍 Most existing hallucination mitigation research focuses on open-source models for post-hoc detection and parameter editing.
🏢 The framework addresses the lack of studies on hallucinations in closed-source LLMs predominantly used in institutional deployments.
2026-02-23  •  William Watson, Nicole Cho, Sumitra Ganesh et al.
What Makes a Good Query? Measuring the Impact of Human-Confusing Linguistic Features on LLM Performance
🧠 Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations are usually treated as defects of the model or its decoding strategy.
📊 Drawing on classical linguistics, we argue that a query's form can also shape a listener's (and model's) response.
⚙️ We operationalize this insight by constructing a 22-dimension query feature vector covering clause complexity, lexical rarity, and anaphora, negation, answerability, and intention grounding, all known to affect human comprehension.
♦
AI Security News
Sat, 28 Feb 2026 17:21:00 GMT  •  The Hacker News
ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
🛡️ OpenClaw fixed a high-severity security flaw, codenamed ClawJacked, in version 2026.2.25 released on February 26, 2026.
🔑 The vulnerability allowed malicious websites to brute-force a local OpenClaw gateway password due to a missing rate-limiting mechanism.
🚨 The flaw exploited local WebSocket connections to silently auto-approve new device registrations without requiring user confirmation.

LLM firewalls emerge as a new AI security layer
🔒 LLM firewalls are specialized tools designed to monitor, filter, and sanitize user input for AI-driven risks like prompt injection attacks and data leaks.
🧠 These firewalls differ from WAFs by analyzing the semantics, intent, and context of natural language in both incoming prompts and outgoing responses.
📐 An LLM firewall typically features a prompt firewall, a retrieval firewall for external data, and a response firewall for outbound generated text.
